enrichment.short_description: Dover pairs a free ATS with a marketplace of fractional recruiters and AI sourcing tools to help startups hire faster.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Dover offers a free ATS, AI sourcing tools, and a vetted fractional-recruiter marketplace built for startups hiring at scale.

features.title: Free ATS
features.description: Unlimited users and jobs, posting to 100+ job boards, and Gmail/Outlook integration at no cost.

features.title: AI applicant scoring
features.description: AI reviews and scores incoming resumes against role requirements to help teams prioritize candidates.

features.title: AI-generated job content
features.description: Generates careers pages and job descriptions automatically as part of the ATS workflow.

features.title: Recruiter marketplace
features.description: Matches startups with vetted fractional recruiters and shows transparent, upfront cost-per-hire pricing.

features.title: Sourcing tools
features.description: Includes a LinkedIn extension for outreach and employee-referral tracking for internal sourcing.

features.title: Agency portal
features.description: Gives hiring teams visibility into candidate pipelines and costs when working with outside recruiters.

pros: Core ATS is free with no user or job limits
Recruiter marketplace shows cost-per-hire pricing upfront instead of hidden agency fees
AI resume scoring and AI note-taking speed up candidate review
Used by 2,000+ companies including Descript and Benchling
cons: Premium ATS tier costs a flat $199/month regardless of team size
Recruiter marketplace fees are on top of any software cost
No public information on company founding date or headquarters

faqs.question: Is Dover's ATS really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Dover offers a free ATS tier with unlimited users and jobs, posting to 100+ job boards, and Gmail/Outlook integration.

faqs.question: What does the Premium ATS add?
faqs.answer: For $199/month, Premium adds unlimited free LinkedIn and X job posts, AI applicant scoring, AI note-taking, onboarding, and premium support.

faqs.question: How does the recruiter marketplace work?
faqs.answer: Dover matches startups with vetted fractional recruiters; recruiters set their own rates and cost breakdowns are shown before engagement.

faqs.question: Who uses Dover?
faqs.answer: Dover is built for startups from seed through Series C+ that want to hire without bringing on a full-time in-house recruiter.

use_cases.title: Free applicant tracking for early-stage startups
use_cases.description: Teams use Dover's free ATS to post jobs, track candidates, and manage a hiring pipeline without software cost.
use_cases.audience: Early-stage startup founders and hiring managers

use_cases.title: On-demand recruiter support
use_cases.description: Growing companies tap Dover's marketplace to bring on fractional recruiters for specific roles with transparent pricing.
use_cases.audience: Startups scaling headcount without a full recruiting team

features: Unlimited free LinkedIn and X job posts

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Dover?
paragraphs: Dover is a hiring platform for startups that combines a free applicant tracking system with a marketplace of fractional recruiters and AI-powered sourcing tools.

The free ATS lets teams post to more than 100 job boards, track candidates, and use AI to score resumes and draft job descriptions, while the recruiter marketplace connects companies with vetted recruiters at transparent, disclosed rates.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Dover is for
paragraphs: Dover is aimed at startups from seed through Series C+ that need to hire quickly but don't want to staff a full internal recruiting team, using the free ATS for day-to-day tracking and the marketplace when extra recruiting help is needed.

enrichment.short_description: Metaview is an agentic AI recruiting platform that sources candidates, reviews applications, and takes interview notes.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Metaview uses AI agents to source candidates, review applications, and capture interview notes for recruiting teams.

features.title: Sourcing Agent
features.description: Autonomously finds candidates and sends outreach based on a defined hiring brief.

features.title: Application review
features.description: AI reviews inbound applications against set criteria and surfaces top matches for recruiters.

features.title: Notetaker
features.description: Automatically captures structured notes from interviews.

features.title: Reports
features.description: A customizable reporting engine that surfaces talent insights and hiring trends.

features.title: Job posts
features.description: Built-in job posting functionality within the platform.

features.title: Fillmore
features.description: An autonomous AI coworker that finds, contacts, and schedules screening calls with candidates.

pros: Free tier available to try AI sourcing with no commitment
Integrates with major ATS platforms including Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, Gem, and SmartRecruiters
Learns from past hiring decisions to refine candidate profiles
Includes enterprise security features like audit trails and compliance alerts
cons: Sourcing agent pricing scales per user, which can add up for larger teams
Full agentic recruiting platform (beyond sourcing) requires custom Enterprise pricing
No public information on founding year or headquarters

faqs.question: Does Metaview have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sourcing Agent Free tier includes the first 100 profiles sourced per month at no cost.

faqs.question: What does the Pro sourcing plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro tier is $100/month per user for 200 sourced profiles per month with infinite concurrent searches.

faqs.question: What ATS platforms does Metaview integrate with?
faqs.answer: Metaview integrates with ATS platforms including Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, Gem, and SmartRecruiters.

faqs.question: What is Fillmore?
faqs.answer: Fillmore is Metaview's autonomous AI coworker that finds candidates, reaches out, and schedules screening calls without manual intervention.

use_cases.title: Automated candidate sourcing
use_cases.description: Recruiters use the Sourcing Agent to continuously find and contact candidates that match a hiring brief.
use_cases.audience: In-house recruiters and executive search partners

use_cases.title: Interview documentation
use_cases.description: Hiring teams use the Notetaker to automatically capture structured notes during interviews.
use_cases.audience: Hiring managers and interview panels

features: Sourcing, Application Review, Notes, and Reports agents

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Metaview?
paragraphs: Metaview is an agentic AI recruiting platform built to automate hiring workflows from sourcing through interview notes and reporting.

Its AI agents include a Sourcing Agent that finds and contacts candidates, an Application Review agent that scores inbound applicants, a Notetaker that captures interview notes, and Fillmore, an agent that schedules screening calls autonomously.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Metaview is for
paragraphs: Metaview targets recruiters, hiring managers, and executive search partners at companies ranging from startups to Fortune 500 organizations who want to automate repetitive parts of the recruiting workflow.

enrichment.short_description: Paraform connects companies with a network of 10,000+ specialized recruiters supported by custom AI matching agents.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Paraform pairs companies with specialized recruiters and AI agents to fill open roles faster across industries.

features.title: Recruiter network
features.description: Access to more than 10,000 specialized recruiters with track records in specific hiring needs.

features.title: Custom AI agents
features.description: AI agents that learn from each hiring cycle to improve candidate matching over time.

features.title: White-glove support
features.description: Dedicated talent strategists manage engagements on an ongoing basis.

features.title: Recruiter toolkit
features.description: A free AI toolkit for recruiters covering sourcing, candidate matching, CRM, note-taking, and scheduling.

features.title: Back-office support
features.description: Paraform handles client communications, feedback collection, and payment processing between companies and recruiters.

pros: Recruiter network spans legal, engineering, and enterprise specialties
Notable clients include Palantir, Rippling, Cognition AI, and Scale AI
Reports a 98% satisfaction rate and a 70% interview rate for placements
Recruiter toolkit is free to use for independent and agency recruiters
cons: No public pricing is listed for companies using the platform
Model depends on working through third-party recruiters rather than self-serve software
No public information on founding year, headquarters, or company size

faqs.question: How does Paraform match recruiters to roles?
faqs.answer: Paraform uses custom AI agents that learn from each hiring cycle to connect companies with recruiters who have a track record in similar roles.

faqs.question: Is Paraform's toolkit free for recruiters?
faqs.answer: Yes, Paraform provides a free AI toolkit for recruiters covering sourcing, candidate matching, CRM, note-taking, and scheduling.

faqs.question: What companies use Paraform?
faqs.answer: Paraform's stated clients include Palantir, Rippling, Cognition AI, Scale AI, Thomson Reuters, and Basis.

faqs.question: Does Paraform publish pricing?
faqs.answer: No, pricing is not published on Paraform's website; details are handled directly between companies and recruiters on the platform.

use_cases.title: Specialized role hiring
use_cases.description: Companies use Paraform to reach recruiters with proven experience filling legal, engineering, or executive roles.
use_cases.audience: Startups and enterprises hiring for specialized positions

use_cases.title: Recruiter deal flow
use_cases.description: Independent and agency recruiters use Paraform's free toolkit to find open roles and manage candidates.
use_cases.audience: Independent and agency recruiters

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Paraform?
paragraphs: Paraform is a hiring platform that connects companies with a network of more than 10,000 specialized recruiters, supported by custom AI agents that learn from each hiring cycle to improve candidate matching.
The platform also gives recruiters a free AI toolkit for sourcing, CRM, note-taking, and scheduling, while Paraform's back office manages client communication and payments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Paraform is for
paragraphs: Paraform serves companies from early-stage startups to enterprises that need specialized recruiting help, as well as independent and agency recruiters looking for deal flow and tooling.

enrichment.short_description: Juicebox (PeopleGPT) lets recruiters search 800M+ candidate profiles using natural language and automate outreach.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Juicebox's PeopleGPT search lets recruiters find candidates across 800M+ profiles with natural language queries.

features.title: PeopleGPT search
features.description: Search across 800M+ profiles from 30+ sources using natural language queries with enriched candidate profiles.

features.title: AI agents
features.description: Automated agents that continuously search for candidates, learn from hiring patterns, and run outreach around the clock.

features.title: Talent insights
features.description: Real-time market data and talent pool analytics.

features.title: Automated outreach
features.description: Dynamic, multi-step outreach sequences with personalized messaging.

features.title: ATS/CRM integrations
features.description: Connects with 41 ATS systems and 21 CRMs, including Lever, Greenhouse, Ashby, Workday, and Bullhorn.

features.title: Chrome extension
features.description: A browser extension for sourcing candidates while browsing the web.

pros: Free tier available to try searches before paying
Searches across 800M+ profiles from 30+ sources
Wide ATS/CRM integration coverage (41 ATS, 21 CRMs)
Annual billing offers a discount over monthly pricing
cons: Higher-volume Business tier requires contacting sales for custom pricing
Contact and export credits are capped by plan, which can limit heavy sourcing
Juicebox Agents are a separate $199/agent/month add-on

faqs.question: Is there a free version of Juicebox?
faqs.answer: Yes, Juicebox offers a Free plan with limited free searches and AI email templates.

faqs.question: How much is the Starter plan?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan is $99/month for one seat, with unlimited searches and 500 contact and export credits.

faqs.question: What does the Growth plan include?
faqs.answer: Growth is $179/month for up to 5 seats and adds talent insights, 1,500 contact and export credits, and additional mailboxes.

faqs.question: Does Juicebox integrate with ATS systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, Juicebox integrates with 41 ATS platforms and 21 CRMs, including Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, and Workday.

use_cases.title: Natural-language candidate search
use_cases.description: Recruiters use PeopleGPT to describe a role in plain language and surface matching candidates from 800M+ profiles.
use_cases.audience: In-house recruiters and boutique recruiting agencies

use_cases.title: Automated outbound recruiting
use_cases.description: Teams set up AI agents to continuously source and message candidates without manual follow-up.
use_cases.audience: Talent acquisition teams running high-volume outreach

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Juicebox?
paragraphs: Juicebox, also known as PeopleGPT, is an AI recruiting platform that lets recruiters search more than 800 million candidate profiles across 30+ sources using natural language.
Beyond search, Juicebox offers AI agents that continuously source candidates and run multi-step outreach sequences, plus integrations with 41 ATS platforms and 21 CRMs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Juicebox is for
paragraphs: Juicebox is built for recruiters at organizations of any size, from boutique agencies to large enterprises, who need to find hard-to-source talent and automate candidate outreach.

enrichment.short_description: Findem is an AI talent intelligence platform combining assistive, agentic, and embedded AI for sourcing and analytics.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Findem uses assistive and agentic AI to power sourcing, screening, scheduling, and workforce analytics for talent teams.

features.title: Assistive AI
features.description: AI-assisted sourcing, talent marketing, executive search, analytics, and market intelligence.

features.title: Agentic AI
features.description: Includes an Intelligent Job Post agent, Application Boost Agent, Screening Agent, and Scheduling Agent that plan and execute hiring workflow steps.

features.title: Data Labeling Engine
features.description: Part of Findem's Build AI, used to create AI-ready talent datasets.

features.title: Embedded AI
features.description: Includes an AI Job Board, Sourcing Copilot, and agent/MCP support for embedding Findem's AI elsewhere.

features.title: Success and relationship signals
features.description: Analyzes career history and network connections to evaluate candidate potential beyond a resume.

pros: Combines assistive, agentic, and embedded AI in one platform
Covers the full funnel from sourcing to scheduling with dedicated agents
Used across talent acquisition, executive search, and analytics use cases
cons: No public pricing; requires a demo request to get a quote
No public information on founding year, headquarters, or company size
Specific integration list is not detailed on the main site

faqs.question: What is agentic AI in Findem?
faqs.answer: Findem's agentic AI includes agents such as Intelligent Job Post, Application Boost, Screening, and Scheduling that plan, execute, and improve hiring workflows.

faqs.question: Does Findem publish pricing?
faqs.answer: No, Findem does not publish pricing; prospective customers need to request a demo to receive a quote.

faqs.question: Who uses Findem?
faqs.answer: Findem is used by talent acquisition teams, executive search firms, HR departments, and VC/PE firms, according to its website.

use_cases.title: Talent sourcing and screening
use_cases.description: Talent acquisition teams use Findem's assistive and agentic AI to source, screen, and schedule candidates.
use_cases.audience: In-house talent acquisition teams

use_cases.title: Workforce and market intelligence
use_cases.description: HR and analytics teams use Findem's signals to understand candidate potential and market trends.
use_cases.audience: HR analytics and workforce planning teams

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Findem?
paragraphs: Findem is an AI talent intelligence platform that combines assistive AI, agentic AI, and embedded AI to support sourcing, talent marketing, executive search, and workforce analytics.
Its agentic AI includes agents for job posting, application review, candidate screening, and interview scheduling, while its Data Labeling Engine creates AI-ready talent datasets.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Findem is for
paragraphs: Findem targets talent acquisition teams, executive search firms, HR departments, and investment firms that need AI-driven sourcing and workforce analytics.

enrichment.short_description: hireEZ is an agentic AI recruiting layer that sources, screens, and schedules candidates on top of an existing ATS.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: hireEZ adds agentic AI sourcing, screening, and scheduling on top of your existing ATS without replacing it.

features.title: AI sourcing
features.description: Searches across 45+ external platforms plus internal ATS data to surface candidates.

features.title: Applicant review
features.description: AI-powered candidate matching and shortlisting from inbound applicants.

features.title: Conversational AI screening
features.description: Voice-based phone screening that adapts in real time.

features.title: AI scheduler
features.description: Automated calendar coordination and interview booking.

features.title: Talent CRM
features.description: Nurtures pipelines and resurfaces past applicants for new roles.

features.title: Hiring intelligence
features.description: Market insights and performance reporting for recruiting teams.

pros: Works on top of existing ATS platforms like Workday, iCIMS, Greenhouse, and SAP without a rip-and-replace migration
Used by 70+ Fortune 500 companies according to the vendor
Covers sourcing, screening, and scheduling in one layer
Serves multiple industries including tech, healthcare, and financial services
cons: No public pricing; requires contacting sales
Performance metrics (e.g. time-to-fill reduction) are vendor-reported claims, not independently verified
No public information on founding year or headquarters

faqs.question: Does hireEZ replace my ATS?
faqs.answer: No, hireEZ operates as a layer on top of existing ATS systems such as Workday, iCIMS, Greenhouse, and SAP rather than replacing them.

faqs.question: What does hireEZ's AI sourcing search?
faqs.answer: It searches across more than 45 external platforms in addition to a company's internal ATS data.

faqs.question: Is hireEZ pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, hireEZ does not publish pricing; visitors are directed to contact sales.

faqs.question: What industries use hireEZ?
faqs.answer: hireEZ lists customers across tech/AI, healthcare, staffing/RPO, defense and aerospace, insurance, financial services, manufacturing, and energy.

use_cases.title: Sourcing on top of an existing ATS
use_cases.description: Talent teams use hireEZ to source candidates across external platforms while keeping their current ATS.
use_cases.audience: Talent acquisition leaders and recruiters

use_cases.title: Automated screening and scheduling
use_cases.description: Recruiters use conversational AI screening and the AI scheduler to move candidates through the pipeline faster.
use_cases.audience: Recruiters and hiring managers handling high applicant volume

long_form_blocks.heading: What is HireEZ?
paragraphs: hireEZ is an agentic AI recruiting platform that acts as a 'system of actions' layer on top of an organization's existing ATS, automating sourcing, applicant review, screening, and scheduling.
It searches across 45+ external platforms in addition to internal ATS data, and includes conversational AI for phone screening and an AI scheduler for interview coordination.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who HireEZ is for
paragraphs: hireEZ is aimed at talent acquisition leaders, recruiters, sourcers, and hiring managers at organizations that want to add AI automation without migrating off their current ATS.

enrichment.short_description: SeekOut is an agentic AI recruiting platform that searches 1B+ profiles and automates screening and outreach.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SeekOut searches over 1 billion candidate profiles with AI screening, outreach, and ATS integrations for recruiters.

features.title: Intelligent search
features.description: Context-aware AI search across 1B+ candidate profiles and connected ATS systems.

features.title: AI screening
features.description: Automated video interviews that evaluate candidates against custom criteria.

features.title: Inbound evaluation
features.description: Processes large volumes of applicants with same-day AI scoring.

features.title: Personalized outreach
features.description: AI-crafted, multi-touch candidate engagement campaigns.

features.title: Talent rediscovery
features.description: Surfaces past applicants and silver-medalist candidates from an existing ATS.

features.title: SeekOut MCP
features.description: Integrates SeekOut with AI assistants including Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, and Microsoft Copilot.

pros: 14-day free trial available on the self-serve Core plan with no credit card required
Searches across 1 billion or more candidate profiles
Broad ATS integration list including Workday, Greenhouse, iCIMS, Lever, and Ashby
750+ customers including DocuSign, Sony, and Microsoft, per the vendor
cons: Advanced sourcing, integration, and full-funnel plans require custom quotes
AI screening and evaluation features add complexity that may require setup time
Core self-serve plan pricing is higher on month-to-month billing ($179/month) than annual ($149/month)

faqs.question: Does SeekOut offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SeekOut Recruit Core plan includes a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does SeekOut Recruit Core cost?
faqs.answer: Core is $149/month billed annually, or $179/month billed monthly.

faqs.question: What ATS systems does SeekOut integrate with?
faqs.answer: SeekOut integrates with Workday, Greenhouse, iCIMS, Lever, Ashby, Avature, Beamery, Bullhorn, Gem, Jobvite, Phenom, SAP SuccessFactors, SmartRecruiters, and Talemetry.

faqs.question: What is SeekOut MCP?
faqs.answer: SeekOut MCP lets recruiters access SeekOut's data through AI assistants such as Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, and Microsoft Copilot.

use_cases.title: High-volume candidate sourcing
use_cases.description: Recruiters use SeekOut's search across 1B+ profiles to find candidates for hard-to-fill roles.
use_cases.audience: Individual recruiters and small teams

use_cases.title: Automated inbound screening
use_cases.description: Talent acquisition teams use SeekOut Sam and AI screening to evaluate large volumes of applicants same-day.
use_cases.audience: Enterprise talent acquisition teams handling high applicant volume

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SeekOut?
paragraphs: SeekOut is an agentic AI recruiting platform that helps talent acquisition teams source, screen, and engage candidates across a database of more than 1 billion profiles.

It combines AI-powered search with automated video screening, inbound applicant evaluation, and personalized multi-touch outreach, plus integrations with major ATS systems and AI assistants via SeekOut MCP.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SeekOut is for
paragraphs: SeekOut serves individual recruiters and small teams through its self-serve Core plan, and larger talent acquisition organizations and enterprises through custom Sourcing, Integration, and Full Recruiting Funnel plans.

enrichment.short_description: Leapsome unifies HRIS and talent management, covering performance reviews, engagement surveys, goals, and AI agents.
enrichment.headquarters: New York, NY, USA and Berlin, Germany
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Leapsome combines HRIS, performance reviews, engagement surveys, and AI agents in one modular HR platform.

features.title: HRIS module
features.description: Employee records, documents and e-signatures, absence management, time tracking, onboarding, and payroll prep.

features.title: Talent suite
features.description: Recruiting, performance reviews, engagement surveys, goals and OKRs, learning management, and compensation.

features.title: AI agents
features.description: Generate automations, dashboards, and team insights, and provide personalized employee coaching.

features.title: Continuous feedback and 1:1s
features.description: Tools for ongoing feedback and structured one-on-one meetings.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with 100+ tools including BambooHR, Slack, Workday, HiBob, Deel, Personio, and Greenhouse.

pros: 14-day free trial available with no credit card required
Combines HRIS and talent management in a single platform, reducing tool sprawl
European hosting in Germany with GDPR, ISO 27001:2022, and EU AI Act compliance
Used by 2,000+ organizations including Spotify, Notion, and Sony
cons: Pricing is modular and quote-based rather than fixed self-serve tiers
Minimum 1-year contract length
No public information on founding year or exact company size

faqs.question: How is Leapsome priced?
faqs.answer: Leapsome uses a modular pricing model based on employee count, selected modules, and contract length, with plans starting around €199/month per user.

faqs.question: Does Leapsome offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Leapsome offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Where is Leapsome based?
faqs.answer: Leapsome has offices in New York, NY and Berlin, Germany.

faqs.question: What integrations does Leapsome support?
faqs.answer: Leapsome integrates with 100+ tools including BambooHR, Slack, Workday, HiBob, Deel, Personio, SAP, Greenhouse, Rippling, Microsoft Teams, and ADP.

use_cases.title: Unified HR operations
use_cases.description: Companies replace separate HRIS and performance tools with Leapsome's combined platform.
use_cases.audience: People operations and HR leaders

use_cases.title: Manager and employee coaching
use_cases.description: Managers use Leapsome's AI agents for team insights and personalized coaching during 1:1s.
use_cases.audience: Managers and team leads

features: Choice of HRIS and/or Talent Suite modules
AI Agents, Workflows, and Analytics included

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Leapsome?
paragraphs: Leapsome is an AI-powered HR platform that unifies HRIS functions like employee records and time tracking with talent management tools such as performance reviews, engagement surveys, goals and OKRs, and compensation.
Its AI agents can generate workflow automations and dashboards, surface team insights, and provide personalized coaching for employees and managers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Leapsome is for
paragraphs: Leapsome serves companies of varying sizes, from startups to larger organizations, across industries including tech, e-commerce, fintech, and professional services, that want HRIS and talent management combined in one system.

enrichment.short_description: 15Five is an AI-powered performance management platform for reviews, engagement surveys, and manager coaching.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: 15Five combines performance reviews, engagement surveys, and AI manager coaching in one people management platform.

features.title: AI-assisted performance reviews
features.description: Helps write and structure performance reviews with AI support.

features.title: Engagement surveys
features.description: Employee engagement surveys with predictive analytics on engagement drivers.

features.title: Kona AI assistant
features.description: AI coaching tool that guides managers with real-time recommendations.

features.title: Goals and OKR tracking
features.description: Tools for setting and tracking team and individual goals.

features.title: Compensation insights
features.description: Pay range management and compensation insight tools.

features.title: 1-on-1 meeting tools
features.description: Structured templates and tracking for manager-employee 1:1s.

features.title: Mobile apps
features.description: iOS and Android apps for accessing reviews, check-ins, and feedback on the go.

pros: Transparent, published per-user pricing across three tiers
Serves 3,000+ customers and 250,000+ employees
Mobile apps available for iOS and Android
Combines engagement surveys, reviews, and manager coaching in one platform
cons: No free plan; all tiers require a paid subscription
AI Assistant (Kona), Manager Products, and Compensation are separate add-ons on top of base pricing
No public information on founding year or headquarters

faqs.question: How much does 15Five cost?
faqs.answer: 15Five's published plans are Engage at $4/user/month, Perform at $11/user/month, and Total Platform at $16/user/month, billed annually per user.

faqs.question: Does 15Five have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, 15Five's plans are paid subscriptions; no free tier is listed on its pricing page.

faqs.question: What is Kona?
faqs.answer: Kona is 15Five's AI assistant that provides manager coaching and guidance, available as an add-on.

faqs.question: Is 15Five available on mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes, 15Five offers mobile apps for both iOS and Android.

use_cases.title: Performance reviews and 1:1s
use_cases.description: Managers use 15Five's Perform tier for structured performance reviews, OKRs, and 1-on-1 meetings.
use_cases.audience: Mid-market and enterprise managers

use_cases.title: Employee engagement tracking
use_cases.description: HR teams use 15Five's Engage tier to run surveys and analyze engagement drivers.
use_cases.audience: HR and people operations teams

features: All Engage and Perform features combined

long_form_blocks.heading: What is 15Five?
paragraphs: 15Five is an AI-powered continuous performance management platform that unifies performance reviews, employee engagement surveys, compensation insights, and manager enablement tools.
It offers three published pricing tiers—Engage, Perform, and Total Platform—along with add-ons like the Kona AI assistant for manager coaching, and mobile apps for iOS and Android.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who 15Five is for
paragraphs: 15Five is aimed at mid-market and enterprise organizations that want to combine performance management, engagement tracking, and manager coaching in a single platform.

enrichment.short_description: Culture Amp is an employee experience platform for engagement surveys, performance management, and people analytics.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Culture Amp offers employee engagement surveys, performance reviews, and AI-powered people analytics for HR teams.

features.title: Engage
features.description: Employee surveys, pulse surveys, retention insights, DEI assessments, and benchmarking with AI comment summaries.

features.title: Perform
features.description: Continuous feedback, performance reviews, 1-on-1 meeting tools, goal management, and 360-degree feedback.

features.title: AI Coach
features.description: Provides instant insights intended to help accelerate performance.

features.title: Performance Culture Quadrant
features.description: An assessment tool for evaluating organizational culture and performance.

features.title: Survey templates and action plans
features.description: Ready-to-use survey templates paired with suggested action plans.

pros: Used by 6,000+ companies including Canva, Etsy, and Intercom, per the vendor
Integrates with major HR SaaS products and HRIS systems
SOC II, ISO, and GDPR compliant
Tiered customer success support based on company size, from group support to dedicated enterprise coaching
cons: No public pricing; requires requesting a quote
Billing is annual, which may not suit companies wanting monthly flexibility
No public information on founding year or specific company size

faqs.question: Does Culture Amp publish pricing?
faqs.answer: No, Culture Amp does not list specific prices; it directs visitors to request a quote based on employee count, products selected, and service tier.

faqs.question: What products does Culture Amp offer?
faqs.answer: Culture Amp's main products are Engage for surveys and engagement, Perform for performance management, and Develop as an add-on for talent growth.

faqs.question: What compliance certifications does Culture Amp have?
faqs.answer: Culture Amp is SOC II, ISO, and GDPR compliant.

faqs.question: Does Culture Amp integrate with other HR systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, Culture Amp states it integrates with every major HR SaaS product in the market, including HRIS data synchronization and Slack/Microsoft Teams integration.

use_cases.title: Employee engagement surveys
use_cases.description: HR teams use Culture Amp's Engage product to run pulse surveys and analyze retention risk.
use_cases.audience: HR leaders and people teams

use_cases.title: Performance management
use_cases.description: Managers use Culture Amp's Perform product for continuous feedback, reviews, and goal tracking.
use_cases.audience: Managers and employees at mid-to-large organizations

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Culture Amp?
paragraphs: Culture Amp is an employee experience platform that combines engagement surveys, performance management, and people analytics to help organizations build and sustain company culture.
Its Engage product covers surveys, pulse checks, and DEI assessments, while Perform covers continuous feedback, reviews, and goal management, with an AI Coach providing insights across both.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Culture Amp is for
paragraphs: Culture Amp serves HR executives, managers, and employees at organizations across professional services, financial services, software, and manufacturing, with service tiers scaled to company size.
